name: Smith Machine Drag Curl
resistance_profile: The fixed vertical bar path lets you drag the bar up the torso with the elbows travelling back, holding tension on the biceps through the contracted range and emphasizing the long head.
cues: Drag the bar up your shirt, keeping it in contact with the body
Let the elbows slide back, never forward
Squeeze at the top before reversing
execution_steps: Set the Smith bar to roughly hip height and grip it shoulder-width with palms facing up.
Stand close so the bar rests against your thighs, chest up and shoulders back.
Curl by dragging the bar straight up along your torso, letting your elbows slide backward behind your body.
Stop when the bar reaches your lower chest and squeeze the biceps hard.
Lower the bar back down the same path under control until the arms are fully extended.
good_when: You want to remove swinging with a fixed bar path
Emphasizing the long head with elbows-back mechanics
You struggle to keep a free-barbell drag-curl path honest
bad_when: You want a full-ROM loaded stretch on the biceps
The fixed bar path irritates your wrists or elbows
common_errors: Pushing the elbows forward and turning it into a normal curl
Using leg drive to start the bar moving
progressions: Add a pause in the top contracted position
regressions: Shorten the range and focus on the squeeze
